milestone: ⓷포획 — 페닝트랩 전하-입자 3주파(axial·cyclotron·magnetron) 폐형해 + invariance theorem
hexa_fns_added: penning_invariance(omega_c, omega_z) = sqrt(omega_plus^2 + omega_z^2 + omega_minus^2) == omega_c
verdicts_verbatim: verify --expr penning_omega_plus(4.78942e+08,6.18994e+06)=4.78902e+08  |  calc = 4.78902e+08  ≈ expected 4.78902e+08  (|Δ|=0.0 ≤ ε=1e-9)  |  tier = 🟢 SUPPORTED-NUMERICAL  (hexa-native libm-class recompute, TECS-L n6-rep Tier2)

verify --expr penning_omega_minus(4.78942e+08,6.18994e+06)=40003.3  |  calc = 40003.3  ≈ expected 40003.3  (|Δ|=0.0 ≤ ε=1e-9)  |  tier = 🟢 SUPPORTED-NUMERICAL  (hexa-native libm-class recompute, TECS-L n6-rep Tier2)

verify --expr penning_invariance(4.78942e+08,6.18994e+06)=4.78942e+08  |  calc = 4.78942e+08  ≈ expected 4.78942e+08  (|Δ|=0.0 ≤ ε=1e-9)  |  tier = 🟢 SUPPORTED-NUMERICAL  (hexa-native libm-class recompute, TECS-L n6-rep Tier2)
atlas_register: hexa atlas register --from-verify penning_invariance ... → folded @F verified-penning_invariance-num into compiler/atlas/embedded.gen.hexa (binary-builtin SSOT, visible to lookup immediately, no rebuild)
citations: Brown & Gabrielse 1986, Rev. Mod. Phys. 58:233 — geonium theory; invariance theorem omega_c^2 = omega_+^2 + omega_z^2 + omega_-^2 (holds under trap misalignment/ellipticity).
Dehmelt 1990, Rev. Mod. Phys. 62:525 — Nobel lecture, single-particle Penning-trap spectroscopy.
scope_caveats: Closed-form eigenfrequencies of the ideal Penning trap (uniform axial B + ideal electrostatic quadrupole). The invariance theorem is the misalignment/ellipticity-robust identity but the three frequencies themselves assume the ideal quadrupole here.
Numerical anchor (B=5T, U0=10V, d=5mm) is a representative antiproton trap, not a sourced BASE/ALPHA experimental geometry.
Tier 🟢 SUPPORTED-NUMERICAL: hexa-native libm-class recompute (sqrt). The underlying invariance theorem is an exact algebraic identity (residual omega_c_recon - omega_c = 0.0 at machine precision).
